ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Date and time when the tweet was posted */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text content of the tweet */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the tweet */,
  "lang" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "tweet_type" TEXT /* Type of tweet (e.g., original, retweet, reply) */,
  "sentiment" TEXT /* Sentiment of the tweet (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "entities" TEXT /* Entities mentioned in the tweet (e.g., hashtags, mentions, links) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_username FOREIGN KEY ("username") REFERENCES users ("username")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biography of the user */,
  "followers" INTEGER /* Number of followers */,
  "following" INTEGER /* Number of accounts the user is following */,
  "account_type" TEXT /* Type of account (e.g., personal, organization) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hashtags" (
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hashtag */,
  "hashtag" TEXT /* Hashtag used in tweets */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the hashtag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hashtag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_hashtags" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* ID of the hashtag used in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id", "hashtag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "links" (
  "link_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each link */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the link */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the link */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the link */,
  "domain" TEXT /* Domain of the link */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("link_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_links" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "link_id" INTEGER /* ID of the link mentioned in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id", "link_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mentions" (
  "mention_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mention */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the mentioned user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the mentioned user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mention_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_mentions" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "mention_id" INTEGER /* ID of the mentioned user in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id", "mention_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "organizations" (
  "organization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each organization */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the organization */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the organization */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the organization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("organization_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_organizations" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "organization_id" INTEGER /* ID of the organization mentioned in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id", "organization_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the event or incident */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event or incident */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the event or incident */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_events" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event or incident mentioned in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id", "event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysts" (
  "analyst_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analyst */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the analyst */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the analyst */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the analyst (e.g., researcher, data analyst) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the analyst (e.g., cybersecurity, data science) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analyst_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analyst_tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "analyst_id" INTEGER /* ID of the analyst who analyzed the tweet */,
  "analysis" TEXT /* Analysis of the tweet by the analyst */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id", "analyst_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that triggered the alert */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reporting" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that is part of the report */,
  "analyst_id" INTEGER /* ID of the analyst who generated the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Report data (e.g., metrics, insights) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reporting_analyst_id FOREIGN KEY ("analyst_id") REFERENCES analysts ("analyst_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reporting_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clients" (
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each client */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the client */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the client */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the client */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "client_alerts" (
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alert sent to the client */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("client_id", "alert_id")
)
